channel.title: NPR Topics: World
channel.description: NPR world news, international art and culture, world business and financial markets, world economy, and global trends in health, science and technology. Subscribe to the World Story of the Day podcast and RSS feed.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Trump says deal to end Iran war will be signed Sunday, as Iran disagrees on timing
item.description: Pakistan's prime minister, a key mediator in U.S.-Iran talks to end the war, said Saturday that a peace deal was closer "than ever before," and could be finalized "in the next 24 hours."
item.content:encoded:
Pakistan's prime minister, a key mediator in U.S.-Iran talks to end the war, said Saturday that a peace deal was closer "than ever before," and could be finalized "in the next 24 hours."

(Image credit: Razieh Poudat)
item.dc:creator: Emma Bowman

item.title: World Cup online betting takes a toll in Brazil
item.description: A World Cup fever is sweeping Brazil. So is sports betting. NPR's Julia Carneiro reports on the country's gambling boom and the growing concerns about debt and addiction.
item.content:encoded: A World Cup fever is sweeping Brazil. So is sports betting. NPR's Julia Carneiro reports on the country's gambling boom and the growing concerns about debt and addiction.
item.dc:creator: Júlia Dias Carneiro

item.title: A Lebanon town's grief in the aftermath of a deadly Israeli airstrike
item.description: More than 3,700 people in Lebanon have died in the war between Israel and Hezbollah. In a village in southern Lebanon, one airstrike last month killed 14 people, including 10 women and children.
item.content:encoded: More than 3,700 people in Lebanon have died in the war between Israel and Hezbollah. In a village in southern Lebanon, one airstrike last month killed 14 people, including 10 women and children.
item.dc:creator: Jane Arraf

item.title: An exiled Russian journalist starts new career as a comedian
item.description: Exiled Russian journalist Vladimir Raevsky says laughter can be a weapon against authoritarianism. NPR's Michele Kelemen reports on his history-inspired comedy act.
item.content:encoded: Exiled Russian journalist Vladimir Raevsky says laughter can be a weapon against authoritarianism. NPR's Michele Kelemen reports on his history-inspired comedy act.
item.dc:creator: Michele Kelemen

item.title: Trump travels to France for the 52nd G7 Summit
item.description: President Trump heads to France for the G7 as the war in Iran continues to dominate global discussions.
item.content:encoded: President Trump heads to France for the G7 as the war in Iran continues to dominate global discussions.
item.dc:creator: Franco Ordoñez

item.title: Pope Leo XIV's flight home from Spain was grounded so the king came to his aid
item.description: Leo's Iberia charter, due to take him back to Rome after a weeklong visit to Spain, was grounded by a technical problem Friday, prompting Spain's king to offer his private jet instead.
item.content:encoded:
Leo's Iberia charter, due to take him back to Rome after a weeklong visit to Spain, was grounded by a technical problem Friday, prompting Spain's king to offer his private jet instead.

(Image credit: Vatican Media via AP)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Trump says U.S. military strike killed leader of Tren de Aragua gang
item.description: President Trump said Friday that a U.S. strike has killed Hector Rusthenford Guerrero Flores, whom he called "the infamous leader" of the Tren de Aragua gang in Venezuela.
item.content:encoded:
President Trump said Friday that a U.S. strike has killed Hector Rusthenford Guerrero Flores, whom he called "the infamous leader" of the Tren de Aragua gang in Venezuela.

(Image credit: Jacquelyn Martin)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: China arrests a U.S. scholar with a history of Myanmar activism, suspected of spying
item.description: China's government said Min Zin, who heads a think tank focused on Myanmar, was detained on suspicion of engaging in "espionage and endangering Chinese national security."
item.content:encoded:
China's government said Min Zin, who heads a think tank focused on Myanmar, was detained on suspicion of engaging in "espionage and endangering Chinese national security."

(Image credit: AFP via Getty Images)
item.dc:creator: Wai Moe

item.title: Trump cancels further Iran strikes. And, U.S. men's soccer takes on Paraguay
item.description: Trump canceled new Iran strikes and signaled that a peace deal could come soon, but Iran says it hasn't been finalized. And, the U.S. men's soccer team plays its first 2026 World Cup match today.
item.content:encoded:
Trump canceled new Iran strikes and signaled that a peace deal could come soon, but Iran says it hasn't been finalized. And, the U.S. men's soccer team plays its first 2026 World Cup match today.

(Image credit: Amirhosein Khorgooi)
item.dc:creator: Suzanne Nuyen

item.title: She waited decades for Scotland to make the World Cup. At 93, she'll cheer in person
item.description: Moira Brown, perhaps the oldest of Scotland's Tartan Army of soccer fans, will be in Boston when Scotland's team plays against Haiti on June 13. "I'm the luckiest person in this world," she says.
item.content:encoded:
Moira Brown, perhaps the oldest of Scotland's Tartan Army of soccer fans, will be in Boston when Scotland's team plays against Haiti on June 13. "I'm the luckiest person in this world," she says.

(Image credit: Lauren Frayer)
item.dc:creator: Lauren Frayer
